meta.description: Concurrency control for command center operations. Two complementary mechanisms: epoch for optimistic state changes, locks for exclusive destructive/billing operations.
meta.applies_to: command-center, check-in groups, bookings, queue entries

epoch.description: Optimistic concurrency control. Prevents stale-state operations without blocking.
epoch.field: epoch (integer, incremented on any mutation to parent or its child check-in groups)

mechanism.poll: Thin clients poll for current epoch on their visible records
mechanism.mismatch: If client epoch < server epoch, client refreshes data before allowing further actions
mechanism.request_validation: Every mutation request includes the client's last-known epoch. Server rejects if epoch < current. Client must refresh and retry.
mechanism.increment: Any mutation to the parent or any child check-in group increments the parent epoch

applies_to_actions: Convert queue entry to check-in group
epoch.notes: Epoch is lightweight. No blocking, no coordination. Operators working on different bookings never interfere with each other.

lock.description: Pessimistic concurrency control. Exclusive access for operations that affect billing, scheduling, or record integrity.

fields.locked_by_name: string | null (for display: 'This booking is being edited by Sarah')
fields.lock_type: string | null (refund, reschedule, edit, delete — for display context)

timeout.behavior: Lock auto-releases when locked_at + timeout has passed. Server checks on every request.

release.automatic: On save or cancel of the locking operation
release.manual: Lock owner can release explicitly. Admin can break any lock.

when_locked.operator_actions: Blocked. Message: 'This booking is being edited by [name]. [lock_type] in progress.'
when_locked.admin_actions: Can break lock and take over.
when_locked.waiver_submissions: Allowed. Waivers are additive and don't conflict with billing operations.

lock.notes: Lock is acquired explicitly when an editor/admin begins a critical operation. Not acquired on every edit — only on operations listed above.

interaction.description: Epoch and lock work together. They are not alternatives.

operator_advancing_state: Client sends advance request with epoch
Server checks: is parent locked? If yes, reject with lock message
Server checks: does epoch match? If no, reject with stale data message
Other clients detect epoch change on next poll, refresh
editor_rescheduling: Editor initiates reschedule — client requests lock
Server checks: is parent already locked? If yes, reject with lock holder info
Server acquires lock (locked_by, locked_at, lock_type=reschedule), increments epoch
Other clients detect epoch change, refresh, see lock indicator
Server releases lock, increments epoch again
Other clients detect epoch change, refresh, lock indicator gone
admin_refunding_while_operator_tries_adv: Operator tries to advance state on a check-in group under this booking
Server rejects: parent is locked by [admin name], refund in progress
Admin completes refund, lock releases, epoch increments

POST /acquire.description: Acquire lock on a parent entity

POST /release.description: Release lock. Must be lock owner or admin.

POST /break.description: Force-break a lock held by another user

GET /status/[parent_type]/[parent_id].description: Check lock status on a parent entity

ui_implications.lock_indicator: When a record is locked, all clients see a lock icon with the holder's name and operation type. Operator controls for that booking's check-in groups are disabled.
ui_implications.stale_data_toast: When epoch mismatch detected, client shows a brief notification and auto-refreshes the affected record. Not a full page reload — just the stale entity.
ui_implications.poll_interval: Configurable. Recommend 5-10 seconds for command center. Longer for non-operational views.
ui_implications.admin_break_lock: Confirmation dialog: 'Sarah is currently processing a refund on this booking. Breaking the lock may cause data inconsistency. Continue?'
